interview_transcript.content: Good evening, ma'am. I'm Officer Johnson with the Sacramento Police Department. I'm here in response to a call about a possible disturbance at this address. Before we begin, I want you to know that you are safe now and I'm here to help. Can you please state your full name and date of birth for the record?

(nervously) Oh, thank you, Officer Johnson. My name is Maria Rodriguez, and my date of birth is... (pauses, trying to compose herself) ...February 12, 1985. (sniffling) I'm just really scared right now. What's going to happen to my kids? And... and Juan? (starting to tear up)

Ms. Rodriguez, I understand you're frightened, and that's completely understandable. I want to help ensure the safety of you and your children. Take a deep breath. Can you tell me what happened tonight? Who is Juan, and what is your relationship with him?

(taking a deep breath, trying to calm down) Okay... Juan is my husband. We've been married for five years. (pausing, collecting her thoughts) Tonight... he just lost control. Again. We were arguing, and it escalated. He started yelling at me, and then he grabbed a kitchen knife. (shuddering at the memory) He was waving it at me, threatening me. I was so scared. I thought he was going to hurt me, or the kids. (starting to cry again) My kids, Emily and Michael, they're only 7 and 9 years old. They shouldn't have to see their father like that. (sobbing) He's been like this before, Officer. This isn't the first time. But tonight was the worst. He's never used a knife before. (shaking her head) I don't know what would have happened if you hadn't arrived when you did.

I hear you, Ms. Rodriguez. You're doing the right thing by talking to me. I need to ask you some important questions. Where is Juan right now? And can you describe what he looked like - what was he wearing tonight?

(sniffling) He's... he's in custody, right? I saw the officers take him away. (looking around, trying to make sure) He was wearing a white t-shirt and jeans. He had a bit of a stubble, and his hair was messy. (shuddering at the memory) He was so angry, his eyes were... (trailing off, trying to compose herself) And he had a cut on his hand, I think from when he broke the front door. (pointing to the damaged door) He kicked it in when I tried to lock him out. (starting to tear up again) I was trying to protect the kids, but he just kept coming.

Thank you. Did Juan physically touch you or harm you in any way tonight - hit, push, grab, or otherwise make physical contact? I want to make sure you don't have any injuries that need medical attention.

(wincing, touching her neck) Yes... he did. He grabbed me, and he... (pausing, struggling to continue) he tried to strangle me. I thought I was going to pass out. (showing the officer her neck, where there are visible marks) See? He squeezed my neck so hard. And he also hit me in the face. (touching her cheek, where there's a bruise) I think I might have a cut on my lip too. (sniffling) It hurts to talk, but I'm okay, I think. (looking up at the officer, scared) But I'm really scared of what he might do next. He said he would hurt me, and the kids, if I ever tried to leave him again. (starting to cry again) I'm so afraid of him, Officer.

Ms. Rodriguez, I can see the marks on your neck. It's important that you receive medical attention. I'm noting your visible injuries for the record. Can you tell me approximately what time tonight's incident began? And can you tell me what started the argument?

(shaking her head, trying to remember) It was around... 10:45, I think. We were arguing about something stupid, but it just escalated. He's been getting more and more controlling lately, and I just can't take it anymore. (sniffling) He was yelling at me because I didn't cook his dinner the way he likes it. It sounds ridiculous, but it's just... it's always something with him. (looking down, ashamed) I'm so tired of walking on eggshells around him, never knowing what's going to set him off. (pausing, collecting her thoughts) But tonight, it was different. He was more angry than I've ever seen him before. And when I tried to stand up for myself, he just... (trailing off, remembering the fear) he just lost control.

You mentioned this has happened before. Have there been previous incidents where Juan was physically violent with you? Were any prior incidents ever reported to police? And is there any history of protective or restraining orders between the two of you?

(nodding, tears welling up in her eyes) Yes, there have been other incidents. He's hit me before, and pushed me, and... (pausing, taking a deep breath) and threatened me. I've tried to leave him before, but he always finds a way to convince me to come back. (shaking her head) I'm so ashamed of it, Officer. I feel like I've failed somehow. (sniffling) But I've had enough. I really have.

(Composing herself) Yes, I did report one incident to the police before. He was arrested for domestic battery, but... (sighing) he just got a slap on the wrist. He was supposed to go to counseling, but I don't think he ever did. (looking up at the officer) And yes, there is a restraining order in place. I got it after the last incident, but... (shaking her head) he just ignores it. He says it's just a piece of paper, that it doesn't mean anything. (fear creeping into her voice) But it's not just a piece of paper, Officer. It's my protection. And he's not supposed to be near me, but... (trailing off, remembering the events of the night) but he was here tonight.

This is very important - you are not at fault here. Can you tell me: where are your children Emily and Michael right now, are they safe? And were there any witnesses to tonight's incident besides you and Juan?

(exhaling a sigh of relief) Oh, thank you, Officer. Just saying that... it means a lot to me. (smiling slightly) My children, Emily and Michael, they're safe. The officers took them to a safe place, I think. I don't know where, exactly, but I'm just glad they're away from him. (tearfully) They didn't deserve to see their father like that. No child should ever have to see their parent like that.

(Composing herself) And yes, there was a witness. Our neighbor, Emily Chen, she lives next door. She heard the commotion and came over to see what was happening. She saw Juan yelling at me and saw him break down the door. (gratefully) She called the police for me. I don't know what would have happened if she hadn't. (smiling slightly) She's been a good friend to me, Officer. She's always looking out for me and the kids.
Can you tell me Juan's full name, date of birth, and physical description - height, weight, approximate age? Also, do you know if Juan had been drinking or using any substances tonight?

(thinking for a moment) Juan's full name is Juan Carlos Hernandez. His date of birth is November 10, 1982. He's about 5'10" tall, and he weighs around 180 pounds. He's 40 years old, I think.

(Pausing, considering the question about substance use) Yes, I think he had been drinking tonight. He had been out with his friends earlier, and when he came home, he smelled like alcohol. He was slurring his words a bit, too. (shaking her head) That's usually when he gets like this, when he's been drinking. It's like he loses all control and becomes a different person. (fearfully) It's scary, Officer. He's not the same person when he's sober.
One final question: when Juan had the kitchen knife, did he actually point it at you or make any stabbing motion? Or was he primarily waving it around? And do you know where the knife is now?

(shuddering at the memory) He pointed it at me, Officer. He held it right up to my throat. (touching her neck again, remembering the feeling of the knife) I thought for sure he was going to... (trailing off, unable to finish the sentence) He was waving it around, too, yelling at me and saying he was going to hurt me. He made a few stabbing motions, but he didn't actually stab me. (shaking her head) I don't know what would have happened if you hadn't arrived when you did.

(Thinking for a moment) I think the police took the knife away when they arrived. I saw one of the officers pick it up and put it in a bag. (relieved) I'm just glad it's gone. I don't want to see it again. (looking up at the officer) I just want to be safe, Officer. I want my kids to be safe. Can you please make sure he doesn't hurt us again?

narrative:
SACRAMENTO POLICE DEPARTMENT
INCIDENT REPORT

Case Type: Domestic Violence / Assault with a Deadly Weapon / Criminal Threats / Strangulation / Violation of Protective Order
Date of Report: 04/28/2026
Reporting Officer: Officer Johnson, Badge #1042
Location of Incident: 1457 Oakwood Drive, Apartment 3B, Sacramento, California

VICTIM INFORMATION:
Name: Maria Rodriguez
Date of Birth: February 12, 1985
Relationship to Suspect: Wife

SUSPECT INFORMATION:
Name: Juan Carlos Hernandez
Date of Birth: November 10, 1982
Age: 40
Height: Approximately 5'10"
Weight: Approximately 180 lbs
Description at time of incident: White t-shirt, blue jeans, stubble, messy hair, cut on right hand
Relationship to Victim: Husband (5 years married)

WITNESS:
Name: Emily Chen
Relationship: Next-door neighbor

NARRATIVE:

On the above date, this officer responded to 1457 Oakwood Drive, Apartment 3B, Sacramento, California, in reference to a possible domestic disturbance reported by a neighbor. The call was received at approximately 2245 hours.

Upon arrival, this officer made contact with the victim, Maria Rodriguez (DOB: 02/12/1985), inside the residence. The victim appeared visibly distressed, was crying, and presented with the following observable injuries: visible red marks and bruising on her neck consistent with manual strangulation, bruising on her left cheek, and a laceration on her lower lip.

This officer conducted a victim interview. The victim stated that the suspect, identified as her husband Juan Carlos Hernandez (DOB: 11/10/1982), arrived at the residence earlier in the evening after being out with friends. The victim stated the suspect smelled strongly of alcohol and appeared to be intoxicated, noting slurred speech.

The victim stated that at approximately 2245 hours, an argument began when the suspect became angry about the manner in which the victim had prepared dinner. The argument escalated. The suspect began yelling and then physically grabbed the victim. The victim stated the suspect placed both hands around her neck and squeezed, causing her to believe she was going to lose consciousness. The victim further stated the suspect struck her in the face with his fist, causing the observed bruising to her left cheek. The victim also sustained a laceration to her lower lip during the assault.

The victim stated the suspect then retrieved a kitchen knife from the kitchen area and brandished it at the victim. The victim stated the suspect held the knife directly to her throat and made verbal threats stating he was going to hurt her and the children. The suspect also made stabbing motions with the knife and continued to threaten the victim.

Prior to or during the altercation, the victim stated she attempted to lock the suspect out of the residence. The suspect forcibly kicked in the front door to gain entry. Property damage to the front door was observed by this officer upon arrival. The suspect was noted to have a laceration on his right hand, consistent with the door breach.

The victim stated the couple's two minor children, Emily Rodriguez (age 7) and Michael Rodriguez (age 9), were present in the residence during the incident. The minor children were subsequently removed to a safe location by responding officers.

The victim disclosed a prior history of domestic violence involving the suspect. The victim stated there have been multiple prior incidents of physical assault, including hitting, pushing, and threatening behavior. The victim stated that a prior incident had been reported to police, resulting in the suspect's arrest for domestic battery. The suspect received no custodial sentence but was ordered to attend counseling, which the victim believes the suspect did not complete.

The victim further stated that a civil protective/restraining order is currently in place against the suspect. The victim stated the suspect is prohibited from being present at the residence. The suspect's presence at 1457 Oakwood Drive, Apartment 3B on this date constitutes a violation of said protective order.

Witness Emily Chen, a neighbor residing in an adjacent unit, was identified. Ms. Chen stated she heard a commotion, came to the victim's door, and observed the suspect yelling at the victim and witnessed the suspect forcibly kick in the front door. Ms. Chen placed the call to 9-1-1.

The suspect, Juan Carlos Hernandez, was taken into custody by responding officers at the scene. The kitchen knife used in the commission of the offense was recovered by officers and secured as evidence.

This officer photographed the victim's injuries, including neck marks, facial bruising, and lip laceration. The victim was advised to seek immediate medical attention for her injuries, particularly due to the strangulation, and was provided with information regarding victim advocacy resources.

- Criminal Threats (PC 422)
- Strangulation (PC 273.5(a) / PC 368 / PC 13700)
- Violation of Protective Order (PC 273.6)
- Vandalism / Forcible Entry (property damage to front door)
